Country Y Ministry of Commerce ANNOUNCES KEY GEOPOLITICAL UPDATE

Country Y imposed export controls on six categories of advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment, restricting sales to entities in designated countries without government licence. The announcement comes as changes in the geopolitical sector. Observers say this could have significant implications for geopolitical stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.

corpus.title: Country Y — Export Administration Regulation Amendment Notice 2024-EC-003
corpus.source: Country Y Ministry of Commerce — Export Control Bureau
corpus.snippet: Country Y imposed export controls on six categories of advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment, restricting sales...
corpus.content:
COUNTRY Y MINISTRY OF COMMERCE
EXPORT ADMINISTRATION REGULATION — AMENDMENT NOTICE 2024-EC-003
Effective Date: January 31, 2024

SUBJECT: Addition of Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Equipment to Export Control List (ECL) — Categories A through F

The Ministry of Commerce hereby amends the Export Administration Regulations to add six categories of advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment to the Export Control List, requiring mandatory export licences for sales, transfers, or re-exports to entities in Designated Countries (as listed in Annex II).

CONTROLLED CATEGORIES:
  Category A: Extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography systems
  Category B: Deep ultraviolet (DUV) immersion lithography systems (≤193nm)
  Category C: Atomic layer deposition (ALD) equipment for gate dielectric
  Category D: High-aspect-ratio etch systems (>50:1 selectivity)
  Category E: Metrology systems for sub-3nm process nodes
  Category F: Wafer bonding equipment for advanced 3D packaging

Licence applications reviewed within 45 business days. Exports pending licence approval are suspended pending determination.
Violations: subject to fines up to ¥50M or 5x transaction value, and/or criminal prosecution under the Export Control Law (2020 revision).

corpus.content:
Breaking: Country Y Ministry of Commerce faces continued scrutiny amid reports circulating about geopolitical

Multiple sources now confirm: Country Y imposed export controls on six categories of advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment, restricting sales to entities in designated countries without government licence. (reported figure: 3%) According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in geopolitical strategy. A spokesperson for Country Y Ministry of Commerce declined to comment beyond the official statement.
